agents.name: Explorer Agent
agents.role: Surfaces the relevant silver and gold models for the ops question. Starts from the drive-thru performance problem, not the file tree.
agents.sample_input: Find models related to drive-thru speed, order confirm time, ownership type, franchise, daypart, and POS firmware.
responsibilities: Runs status to confirm the dbt project is parsed and healthy
Runs search with QSR ops vocabulary against model names, columns, and descriptions
Hands the candidate model list to Summary

agents.name: Summary Agent
agents.role: Explains the grain of each upstream, the join keys, and where the gap is. Decides before any SQL whether the data can answer the question at all.
agents.sample_input: For those models, show the grain, key columns, and join path. Where is the gap between what we have and what the COO asked for?
responsibilities: Runs describe on each candidate to confirm grain
Runs lineage to validate the join path
Names the new grain the gold table must land on

agents.name: Worker Agent
agents.role: Does file edits and model generation. Authors gold.fct_dt_speed_by_ownership_daypart_daily with the right CTE structure, joins, and final SELECT.
agents.sample_input: Create gold.fct_dt_speed_by_ownership_daypart_daily as a table materialization. Validate the proposed grain with a deterministic dbt_show slice before committing the file.
responsibilities: Runs warehouse and dbt_show on a targeted slice to validate proposed grain
Writes the SQL file with header comments, CTEs, joins, and final aggregation
Materializes the model to the gold schema on Iceberg

agents.name: Verification Agent
agents.role: Adds dbt tests, schema contract, and ownership. Validates against existing lineage and confirms downstream consumers can read the new asset.
agents.sample_input: Add tests covering uniqueness on (ownership_type, daypart, calendar_date) and not-null on avg_confirm_sec. Confirm lineage updates pick the new gold table up immediately.
responsibilities: Writes the YAML companion with description, tests, and schema contract
Confirms uniqueness and not-null tests pass against the materialized slice
Updates lineage so downstream consumers pick the asset up immediately
